role: This file is the CSS host frame for a sibling-overlay modal that presents the channel-view graph, delegating the actual graph rendering to DNA's fabric GRAPH component.
capabilities: Modal overlay shell with scrim, centered panel, and open/close visibility transitions
Panel header with title, subtitle, and close button hit-target styling
Scrollable body with safe-area bottom padding and centered graph mount
Error, quiet, and default message text styling
Retry button styling for failed graph loads
Responsive SVG sizing with a definite 600px width capped to the viewport
Overrides for .screen.ar responsive grid to force a single-column, content-height layout inside the graph host
Override of the fixed 390px phone-frame width/height to let the graph fill available space
substance_why: The file implements a complete, token-driven visual frame for the channel-view overlay plus concrete responsive overrides discovered from real viewport bugs.
interactions_present: Scrim backdrop for modal dismissal affordance
interactions_missing: No focus, hover, active, or disabled states for buttons
No keyboard/escape or focus-trap interaction styles
No explicit scrim-click close indicator beyond the backdrop
performance_risks: Fixed-position overlay with a semi-transparent scrim can promote a large compositing layer
Opacity/visibility transitions may cause repaints on low-end devices
Fixed 600px SVG width combined with max-width:100% can trigger reflow on viewport resize
No CSS containment applied to the overlay or panel
reuse_why: The responsive fixes and safe-area framing logic are worth preserving, but the rules should be rebuilt against the new design-system tokens and component boundaries.
decomposition_hints: Extract generic modal chrome (overlay, scrim, panel, header, close) into a shared modal primitive
Move graph-host overrides (.channels-graph .screen.ar, .ar-responsive, svg sizing) to the graph/DNA component stylesheet
Move message and retry utilities to a shared feedback/empty-state component
Keep channel-specific title/subtitle spacing only in the channels module
